tasks.title: GPU Health Check
tasks.description: Monitor GPU utilization, temperature, and memory usage to ensure no anomalies are present. Use system tools to gather diagnostic data.

solution.approach: Identify available system tools for GPU diagnostics. Determine whether NVIDIA's nvidia-smi or other CLI utilities can provide real-time GPU utilization, temperature, and memory usage without requiring custom code.
solution.algorithm: Check if 'nvidia-smi' command is available in the environment by running 'which nvidia-smi' or 'command -v nvidia-smi'.

solution.approach: Design a monitoring strategy using nvidia-smi to extract key metrics: GPU utilization percentage, current temperature (in °C), and used/free memory. Parse the output of 'nvidia-smi --query-gpu=utilization.gpu,temperature.gpu,memory.used,memory.total --format=csv,noheader,nounits' to capture these values in a structured format.
solution.algorithm: Execute CLI command to query specific GPU attributes and format output as CSV for easy parsing.
solution.complexity: O(n) where n is number of GPUs

solution.approach: Run the nvidia-smi diagnostic command to collect live GPU health data. Capture stdout directly without redirection to avoid insecure temporary file handling.
solution.algorithm: Use run_shell_command to execute 'nvidia-smi --query-gpu=utilization.gpu,temperature.gpu,memory.used,memory.total --format=csv,noheader,nounits' and store result in a variable for further processing.

solution.approach: Parse the raw output from nvidia-smi and validate that all expected fields are present and within reasonable thresholds (e.g., temp < 85°C, memory usage not at 100%, GPU utilization not consistently maxed). Log anomalies if detected.
solution.algorithm: Split CSV output by lines and commas; compare each metric against predefined acceptable ranges. Output warnings for any out-of-bounds values.
